---
title: Agents Need a Real R Session
subtitle: mcp-repl, and the limits of shell-only R access
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    css: mcp-repl-useR-lightning.css
    slide-number: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    transition: fade
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: true
suppressWarnings(suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(bslib)
  library(ellmer)
  library(htmltools)
  library(shiny)
  library(shinychat)
  library(webshot2)
}))

asset_dir <- "mcp-repl-useR-lightning-assets"
if (!dir.exists(asset_dir)) {
  dir.create(asset_dir)
}
stopifnot(dir.exists(asset_dir))

tool_call <- function(id, call) {
  stopifnot(
    length(id) == 1,
    is.list(call),
    length(call$name) == 1,
    is.list(call$arguments),
    length(call$intent) == 1
  )

  request <- ContentToolRequest(
    id = id,
    name = call$name,
    arguments = call$arguments
  )
  card <- contents_shinychat(
    ContentToolResult(
      value = "",
      request = request
    )
  )
  card$intent <- call$intent
  card$expanded <- TRUE
  card$show_request <- TRUE
  as.tags(card)
}

bash_command <- function(command) {
  stopifnot(length(command) == 1)
  list(name = "bash", arguments = list(command = command), intent = command)
}

edit_file_command <- function(path, instruction) {
  stopifnot(length(path) == 1, length(instruction) == 1)
  list(
    name = "edit_file",
    arguments = list(path = path, instruction = instruction),
    intent = paste(path, instruction, sep = "\n")
  )
}

chat_tool <- function(call) {
  stopifnot(is.list(call))
  list(type = "tool", call = call)
}

chat_note <- function(text) {
  stopifnot(length(text) == 1)
  list(type = "note", text = text)
}

render_chat <- function(filename, assistant_text, steps) {
  stopifnot(
    length(filename) == 1,
    length(assistant_text) == 1,
    is.list(steps),
    length(steps) >= 1
  )

  rendered_steps <- list()
  tool_index <- 0
  for (step in steps) {
    stopifnot(is.list(step), length(step$type) == 1)
    if (identical(step$type, "tool")) {
      tool_index <- tool_index + 1
      rendered_steps <- c(
        rendered_steps,
        list(tool_call(paste0("call_", tool_index), step$call))
      )
    } else if (identical(step$type, "note")) {
      rendered_steps <- c(
        rendered_steps,
        list(div(class = "reasoning-note", tags$p(step$text)))
      )
    } else {
      stop("unsupported chat step type: ", step$type)
    }
  }

  messages <- list(
    list(
      role = "user",
      content = "Can you explore this dataset and build a first model?"
    ),
    list(
      role = "assistant",
      content = do.call(
        tagList,
        c(
          list(tags$p(assistant_text)),
          rendered_steps
        )
      )
    )
  )

  ui <- page_fillable(
    theme = bs_theme(
      bg = "#141821",
      fg = "#f5f7fb",
      primary = "#315cf6"
    ),
    tags$style(HTML(
      "
      body {
        margin: 0;
        background: #141821;
        font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
      }
      .shot-stage {
        min-height: 100vh;
        display: grid;
        place-items: center;
        padding: 24px;
      }
      .chat-card {
        width: 780px;
        height: 640px;
        background: #f7f8fb;
        border: 1px solid #dce2ec;
        border-radius: 8px;
        padding: 24px;
        box-shadow: 0 30px 90px rgba(0, 0, 0, .30);
      }
      .chat-card shiny-chat-container {
        --bs-body-bg: #f7f8fb;
        --bs-body-color: #172033;
        --bs-body-color-rgb: 23, 32, 51;
        --bs-secondary-color: #626b7a;
        --bs-border-color: #dce2ec;
        --bs-primary: #315cf6;
        --bs-primary-rgb: 49, 92, 246;
        --bs-code-color: #173a9d;
        --shiny-chat-user-message-bg: #315cf6;
        height: 590px;
        color: #172033;
      }
      .chat-card .shiny-chat-user-message {
        color: #ffffff;
      }
      .chat-card .shiny-chat-message:not(.shiny-chat-user-message) {
        color: #172033;
      }
      .chat-card code {
        color: #173a9d;
        background: #edf1ff;
        border-radius: 5px;
        padding: 2px 6px;
      }
      .chat-card shiny-tool-request,
      .chat-card shiny-tool-result {
        margin: .5rem 0;
        font-size: .88rem;
      }
      .chat-card .tool-intent {
        color: #334155;
        font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
        font-size: .82rem;
        font-style: normal;
        opacity: .9;
        white-space: pre-line;
      }
      .chat-card .reasoning-note {
        border-left: 3px solid #aab7cf;
        color: #425069;
        margin: .55rem 0;
        padding: .05rem 0 .05rem .75rem;
      }
      .chat-card .reasoning-note p {
        font-size: .9rem;
        margin: 0;
      }
    "
    )),
    div(
      class = "shot-stage",
      div(
        class = "chat-card",
        chat_ui(
          "chat",
          messages = messages,
          placeholder = "Ask about the R project...",
          width = "100%",
          height = "590px",
          fill = FALSE
        )
      )
    )
  )

  appshot(
    shinyApp(ui, function(input, output, session) {}),
    file = file.path(asset_dir, filename),
    vwidth = 900,
    vheight = 740,
    selector = ".chat-card",
    expand = 4,
    delay = 1
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: chat-screenshots
#| include: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: true
inline_command <- paste(
  "Rscript -e \"library(readr); library(dplyr);",
  "df <- read_csv('sales.csv');",
  "glimpse(df);",
  "summary(df$revenue)\""
)

heredoc_command <- paste(
  c(
    "cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/analysis.R",
    "library(readr)",
    "library(dplyr)",
    "df <- read_csv('sales.csv')",
    "df <- filter(df, !is.na(revenue))",
    "print(glimpse(df))",
    "EOF",
    "Rscript /tmp/analysis.R"
  ),
  collapse = "\n"
)

render_chat(
  "chat-rscript-e.png",
  "I'll inspect the data first, so I'll send R a quick one-off command.",
  list(chat_tool(bash_command(inline_command)))
)

render_chat(
  "chat-heredoc-script.png",
  "I'll inspect the data first, so I'll send R a quick one-off command.",
  list(
    chat_tool(bash_command(inline_command)),
    chat_note("The result gives enough context to take the next step. This is turning into reusable setup, so I should move it into a script."),
    chat_tool(bash_command(heredoc_command))
  )
)

render_chat(
  "chat-rerun-loop.png",
  "Now I'll iterate: change the file, run it, inspect the result, repeat.",
  list(
    chat_tool(bash_command("Rscript /tmp/analysis.R")),
    chat_tool(edit_file_command(
      "/tmp/analysis.R",
      "Add model diagnostics."
    )),
    chat_tool(bash_command("Rscript /tmp/analysis.R"))
  )
)
```

## A simple request

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
::: {.image-frame}
<img src="mcp-repl-useR-lightning-assets/chat-rscript-e.png" alt="A chat interface where a user asks an agent to explore a dataset. The agent responds by running a bash tool call named bash with an inline Rscript command.">
:::
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}

::: {.lede}
`Rscript -e` is a reasonable first move.
:::

::: {.quiet}
One shell call, one immediate result.
:::

:::
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:25.

Even for a larger prompt, this is a natural place to start. The model
calls the shell tool and runs an inline expression with `Rscript -e`.
That is portable, easy to generate, and often good enough for the first
look at the data.
:::

## Then the request has another step

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
::: {.image-frame}
<img src="mcp-repl-useR-lightning-assets/chat-heredoc-script.png" alt="A chat interface where the assistant first uses an inline Rscript bash tool call, then shows a short reasoning summary, then uses another bash tool call to write and run a temporary R script.">
:::
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}

::: {.big-list}
- First tool call.
- Reasoning summary.
- Next tool call.
:::

:::
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:25.

But a typical analysis has more than one step. This slide keeps the
first move in view, then shows a high-level reasoning beat, then the
next tool call. The exact conversation would have more detail, but these
are the touchstones: the agent reads the result, decides the setup now
needs somewhere to live, and writes a temporary script.
:::

## Then iteration keeps that pattern

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
::: {.image-frame}
<img src="mcp-repl-useR-lightning-assets/chat-rerun-loop.png" alt="A chat interface where the assistant runs an Rscript file, edits it with an edit_file tool call, and runs it again.">
:::
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}

```text
run 1: load -> inspect
run 2: load -> clean -> plot
run 3: load -> clean -> fit
run 4: load -> clean -> debug
```

::: {.lede}
The chat remembers.

R starts over.
:::

:::
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:30.

As you ask the model to iterate, explore, and correct itself, it keeps
going in the same direction: edit the file, run the file, inspect the
output, edit the file again. From the user's point of view, the
conversation is continuous. From R's point of view, each attempt is a
fresh process. The pattern has started to expose the mismatch.
:::

## The problem is the shape

<div class="card-grid two">
<div class="card">
<h3>The chat has continuity</h3>
<p>The request, plan, corrections, and intermediate reasoning are all still in view.</p>
</div>

<div class="card">
<h3>The R process does not</h3>
<p>Objects disappear after each run, so the agent rebuilds the world from files.</p>
</div>
</div>

::: {.lede}
We are asking an interactive language to behave like a batch job.
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:25.

This is the core mismatch. The agent is not failing because it is lazy
or careless. It is adapting to the interface we gave it. If the only
primitive is command execution, the agent will build a workflow out of
command execution. But R users already know that the richest work in R
is usually not a sequence of isolated batch jobs.
:::

## R already has the surfaces agents need

```{r}
#| label: r-surfaces-cards
#| message: false
r_surfaces <- list(
  Help = c("?lm", 'help("predict")', "vignette()"),
  Graphics = c("plot(fit)", "ggplot(df, aes(...))"),
  Debugging = c("browser()", "Browse[1]> n", "where"),
  EDA = c("df, fit, res", "str(), ls(), glimpse()")
)

surface_card <- function(title, lines) {
  shiny::tags$div(
    class = "card",
    shiny::tags$h3(title),
    lapply(lines, \(line) shiny::tags$p(shiny::tags$code(line)))
  )
}

cards <- unname(Map(surface_card, names(r_surfaces), r_surfaces))

shiny::tags$div(class = "card-grid", cards)
```

::: {.lede}
These are not just commands. They are a conversation with a live R
session.
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:35.

R has always had rich interactive surfaces: objects that stay in memory,
help pages, vignettes, graphics devices, readline prompts, and the
debugger. Many of these assume a live session. Some need `interactive()`
to be true. Batch execution can approximate parts of this by writing
more files and replaying more setup, but it is an approximation.
:::

## The missing primitive is a REPL

<div class="flow">
  <div class="node">Agent</div>
  <div class="arrow">↔</div>
  <div class="node"><code>repl("...")</code></div>
  <div class="arrow">↔</div>
  <div class="node">Live R session</div>
</div>

<div class="card-grid">
<div class="card"><h3>State persists</h3><p>Create an object once. Inspect it later.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Outputs return</h3><p>Text, images, prompts, errors, and warnings come back through one channel.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Debugging works</h3><p>The agent can step, inspect, and recover inside the same session.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Control is explicit</h3><p>Interrupt when stuck. Reset when a clean slate is better.</p></div>
</div>

::: notes
Timing: ~0:30.

The missing primitive is not a smarter shell command. It is a persistent
REPL: a live R session the agent can use for the length of the task. The
agent should be able to create a model in one call, inspect it in the
next, draw a plot, ask for help, step through `browser()`, interrupt a
bad computation, and reset when needed.
:::

## `mcp-repl` is the small interface around that idea

<div class="card-grid">
<div class="card"><h3>MCP server</h3><p>Runs over stdio, so external agents can attach without a bespoke integration.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3><code>repl</code></h3><p>Send input to the live worker and collect the result.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3><code>repl_reset</code></h3><p>Discard session state and start clean.</p></div>
<div class="card"><h3>Worker runtime</h3><p>Long-lived R or Python process with captured text, images, prompts, and debugger interaction.</p></div>
</div>

::: {.lede}
The public surface is small. The important property is continuity.
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:30.

`mcp-repl` is one implementation of this interface. The MCP tool surface
is intentionally small: send input to the REPL, or reset the session.
Most of the work happens below that surface: worker lifecycle, output
capture, image capture, timeouts, interrupts, prompts, and final
response handling. The effect for the agent is simple: R remains there
between turns.
:::

## Keep the session in a box

```text
Agent-owned R session
  - private runtime state
  - explicit interrupt
  - explicit reset

Sandbox
  - workspace and temp writes only
  - network off unless configured
  - recovery path when the run goes wrong
```

::: {.lede}
Persistence is useful only if the boundary is clear.
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:25.

This is not the same as a human and a model sharing one IDE console. For
agent work, the live session needs a boundary. The model can keep
objects in memory, but writes are constrained, network access is off
unless configured, and the host has explicit controls for interrupt and
reset. The sandbox is part of what makes a persistent session
acceptable.
:::

## This matters when no one is sitting there

<div class="card-grid two">
<div class="card">
<h3>Unattended work</h3>
<ul>
<li>fresh-data reconnaissance</li>
<li>scheduled checks</li>
<li>overnight failure analysis</li>
<li>first-draft reports</li>
</ul>
</div>

<div class="card">
<h3>Workflow evaluation</h3>
<ul>
<li>realistic R tasks</li>
<li>debugger and help use</li>
<li>recovery after errors</li>
<li>regression tests for tool behavior</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>

::: {.lede}
A real session makes the real workflow observable.
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:35.

The same primitive matters beyond a live demo. A human may review the
result, but the session itself can be owned by the agent while work is
happening: scheduled checks, fresh-data reconnaissance, overnight
analysis, or report drafting. It also gives us a better way to evaluate
agents. We can test whether they can inspect state, use help, view
plots, step through the debugger, and recover after mistakes.


<!--
## Two useful shapes
::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

### `mcp-repl`: agent-owned

<div class="flow compact">
  <div class="node">Human review</div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div class="node">Agent</div>
  <div class="arrow">↔</div>
  <div class="node">Private R session</div>
</div>

::: {.split-note}
The human may start or review the work. The agent owns the live runtime.
:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Posit Assistant: human in the loop

<div class="flow compact">
  <div class="node">Human</div>
  <div class="arrow">↔</div>
  <div class="node">Assistant</div>
  <div class="arrow">↔</div>
  <div class="node">IDE R session</div>
</div>

::: {.split-note}
The human, assistant, and R session stay connected inside the IDE.
:::

:::
::: -->



:::
## Two useful shapes

```{r}
#| label: two-session-shapes
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 13.5
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-bg: "#191919"
#| out-width: "100%"
bg <- "#191919"
fg <- "#f6f7fb"
muted <- "#c8d3dc"
panel <- "#2f3944"
code_fill <- "#29323d"
blue <- "#9ee7ff"
blue_muted <- "#6c8795"
blue_fill <- "#17212b"
green <- "#b8f5b0"
green_fill <- "#1f251f"

par(
  mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0),
  pty = "m",
  xaxs = "i",
  yaxs = "i",
  bg = bg,
  family = "sans"
)
plot.new()
plot.window(xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 60))
rect(0, 0, 100, 60, col = bg, border = NA)

x_unit <- par("pin")[1] / diff(par("usr")[1:2])
y_unit <- par("pin")[2] / diff(par("usr")[3:4])
person_radius <- 3.35

draw_box <- function(x0, y0, x1, y1, label, fill, border, cex = 1.05) {
  rect(x0, y0, x1, y1, col = fill, border = border, lwd = 2.6)
  text((x0 + x1) / 2, (y0 + y1) / 2, label, col = fg, cex = cex, font = 2)
}

draw_code <- function(x, y, label, cex) {
  stopifnot(is.character(label), length(label) == 1, cex > 0)
  width <- strwidth(label, units = "user", cex = cex, font = 2, family = "mono")
  height <- strheight(
    label,
    units = "user",
    cex = cex,
    font = 2,
    family = "mono"
  )
  rect(
    x - width / 2 - 0.55,
    y - height / 2 - 0.42,
    x + width / 2 + 0.55,
    y + height / 2 + 0.42,
    col = code_fill,
    border = NA
  )
  text(x, y, label, col = fg, cex = cex, font = 2, family = "mono")
}

draw_inline_code_label <- function(x, y, code, suffix, cex = 1.35) {
  stopifnot(is.character(suffix), length(suffix) == 1)
  code_width <- strwidth(
    code,
    units = "user",
    cex = cex,
    font = 2,
    family = "mono"
  )
  suffix_width <- strwidth(suffix, units = "user", cex = cex, font = 2)
  code_outer_width <- code_width + 1.1
  gap <- 0.12
  left <- x - (code_outer_width + gap + suffix_width) / 2
  draw_code(left + code_outer_width / 2, y, code, cex)
  text(
    left + code_outer_width + gap,
    y,
    suffix,
    col = fg,
    cex = cex,
    font = 2,
    adj = c(0, 0.5)
  )
}

draw_mcp_repl_box <- function(x0, y0, x1, y1, fill, border, cex = 0.95) {
  rect(x0, y0, x1, y1, col = fill, border = border, lwd = 2.6)
  draw_code((x0 + x1) / 2, (y0 + y1) / 2 + 1.45, "mcp-repl", cex = cex)
  text(
    (x0 + x1) / 2,
    (y0 + y1) / 2 - 2.05,
    "R session",
    col = fg,
    cex = cex,
    font = 2
  )
}

draw_person <- function(x, y, border) {
  symbols(
    x,
    y,
    circles = person_radius,
    inches = FALSE,
    add = TRUE,
    fg = border,
    bg = bg,
    lwd = 2.8
  )
  symbols(
    x,
    y + 1.15,
    circles = 0.9,
    inches = FALSE,
    add = TRUE,
    fg = border,
    bg = bg,
    lwd = 2.4
  )
  shoulder_x <- seq(-1.95, 1.95, length.out = 32)
  shoulder_y <- -1.8 + 0.9 * sqrt(pmax(0, 1 - (shoulder_x / 1.95)^2))
  lines(x + shoulder_x, y + shoulder_y, col = border, lwd = 2.4, lend = "round")
  text(x, y - 5.25, "Human", col = fg, cex = 0.8, font = 2)
}

draw_arrow_between <- function(from, to, col, start_gap, end_gap, arrow) {
  stopifnot(length(from) == 2, length(to) == 2)
  dx <- to[1] - from[1]
  dy <- to[2] - from[2]
  data_distance <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  visual_distance <- sqrt((dx * x_unit)^2 + (dy * y_unit)^2)
  stopifnot(
    start_gap >= 0,
    end_gap >= 0,
    arrow$shaft_length > 0,
    arrow$edge_gap >= 0,
    arrow$head_length > 0,
    arrow$lwd > 0,
    is.character(arrow$lty),
    length(arrow$lty) == 1,
    data_distance > 0,
    visual_distance > 0
  )
  start_fraction <- start_gap / data_distance
  end_fraction <- 1 - end_gap / data_distance
  edge_gap_fraction <- arrow$edge_gap / visual_distance
  arrow_start <- start_fraction + edge_gap_fraction
  arrow_end <- end_fraction - edge_gap_fraction
  actual_shaft_length <- visual_distance * (arrow_end - arrow_start)
  stopifnot(
    arrow_start < arrow_end,
    isTRUE(all.equal(
      actual_shaft_length,
      arrow$shaft_length,
      tolerance = 1e-3
    ))
  )
  arrows(
    from[1] + arrow_start * dx,
    from[2] + arrow_start * dy,
    from[1] + arrow_end * dx,
    from[2] + arrow_end * dy,
    length = arrow$head_length,
    angle = 24,
    code = 3,
    col = col,
    lwd = arrow$lwd,
    lty = arrow$lty
  )
}

circle_edge_gap <- function(from, to, radius) {
  dx <- to[1] - from[1]
  dy <- to[2] - from[2]
  data_distance <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  visual_distance <- sqrt((dx * x_unit)^2 + (dy * y_unit)^2)
  stopifnot(visual_distance > 0)
  data_distance * radius * x_unit / visual_distance
}

box_edge_gap <- function(from, to, half_width, half_height) {
  dx <- to[1] - from[1]
  dy <- to[2] - from[2]
  distance <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  stopifnot(distance > 0)
  x_scale <- if (dx == 0) Inf else half_width / abs(dx)
  y_scale <- if (dy == 0) Inf else half_height / abs(dy)
  distance * min(x_scale, y_scale)
}

agent_arrow <- list(
  shaft_length = 0.60,
  edge_gap = 0.08,
  head_length = 0.10,
  lwd = 2.8,
  lty = "solid"
)
optional_agent_arrow <- list(
  shaft_length = 0.60,
  edge_gap = 0.08,
  head_length = 0.10,
  lwd = 2.4,
  lty = "22"
)
ide_arrow <- list(
  shaft_length = 1.00,
  edge_gap = 0.16,
  head_length = 0.11,
  lwd = 2.8,
  lty = "solid"
)

rect(c(2, 52), 7, c(48, 98), 53, border = panel, col = NA, lwd = 1.8)
draw_inline_code_label(25, 49.5, "mcp-repl", ": agent-owned")
text(
  75,
  49.5,
  "Posit Assistant: Human in the loop",
  col = fg,
  cex = 1.35,
  font = 2
)
text(
  c(25, 75),
  c(13, 10.7),
  c(
    "Agent can run unattended.\nHuman only interacts with the Agent.",
    "Human and agent share the same R Session."
  ),
  col = muted,
  cex = c(0.91, 0.98)
)

agent_open <- agent_arrow$shaft_length + 2 * agent_arrow$edge_gap
agent_node <- c(25, 31)
agent_human <- c(
  agent_node[1] - (agent_open / x_unit + person_radius + 7),
  31
)
agent_repl <- c(agent_node[1] + agent_open / x_unit + 7 + 5, 31)
draw_arrow_between(
  agent_human,
  agent_node,
  blue_muted,
  start_gap = circle_edge_gap(agent_human, agent_node, person_radius),
  end_gap = box_edge_gap(agent_node, agent_human, 7, 4.5),
  arrow = optional_agent_arrow
)
draw_arrow_between(
  agent_node,
  agent_repl,
  blue,
  start_gap = box_edge_gap(agent_node, agent_repl, 7, 4.5),
  end_gap = box_edge_gap(agent_repl, agent_node, 5, 6.2),
  arrow = agent_arrow
)
draw_person(agent_human[1], agent_human[2], blue_muted)
draw_box(18, 26.5, 32, 35.5, "Agent", blue_fill, blue)
draw_mcp_repl_box(37, 24.8, 47, 37.2, blue_fill, blue)

ide_open <- ide_arrow$shaft_length + 2 * ide_arrow$edge_gap
triangle_top_y <- 41.5
triangle_left <- c(62, triangle_top_y)
triangle_right <- c(
  triangle_left[1] + ide_open / x_unit + person_radius + 8.2,
  triangle_top_y
)
triangle_bottom <- c(71.70876, 22.78294)

draw_arrow_between(
  triangle_left,
  triangle_right,
  green,
  start_gap = circle_edge_gap(triangle_left, triangle_right, person_radius),
  end_gap = box_edge_gap(triangle_right, triangle_left, 8.2, 4.4),
  arrow = ide_arrow
)
draw_arrow_between(
  triangle_right,
  triangle_bottom,
  green,
  start_gap = box_edge_gap(triangle_right, triangle_bottom, 8.2, 4.4),
  end_gap = box_edge_gap(triangle_bottom, triangle_right, 8.8, 4.2),
  arrow = ide_arrow
)
draw_arrow_between(
  triangle_bottom,
  triangle_left,
  green,
  start_gap = box_edge_gap(triangle_bottom, triangle_left, 8.8, 4.2),
  end_gap = circle_edge_gap(triangle_left, triangle_bottom, person_radius),
  arrow = ide_arrow
)
draw_person(triangle_left[1], triangle_left[2], green)
draw_box(
  triangle_right[1] - 8.2,
  triangle_right[2] - 4.4,
  triangle_right[1] + 8.2,
  triangle_right[2] + 4.4,
  "Assistant",
  green_fill,
  green
)
draw_box(
  triangle_bottom[1] - 8.8,
  triangle_bottom[2] - 4.2,
  triangle_bottom[1] + 8.8,
  triangle_bottom[2] + 4.2,
  "IDE R session",
  green_fill,
  green,
  cex = 0.98
)
```

::: notes
Timing: ~0:35.

These are related, but they are not the same product shape. `mcp-repl`
is agent-facing. It gives external agents a private R session that can
run without a human directly attached to the console. Posit Assistant is
the richer interactive path inside the IDE, where the human, the
assistant, and the R session are all part of the same environment.
:::

## Takeaway

::: {.takeaway}
If an agent is going to work in R, it should get a live R session, not
only a shell.
:::

`mcp-repl` is the open-source, agent-facing path: persistent,
interactive, private, and sandboxed.

For human-in-the-loop interactive analysis, the integrated IDE path is
Posit Assistant.

::: {.small}
<https://github.com/posit-dev/mcp-repl>
:::

::: notes
Timing: ~0:20.

The takeaway is narrow. R support for agents should not stop at command
execution. For agent-owned sessions, `mcp-repl` gives R a persistent
interactive surface over MCP. For interactive work with a human in the
loop, Posit Assistant is the integrated IDE path.
:::
